lede: A look at January 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including J. R. R. Tolkien and Cicero, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States
events.desc: The United States conducts airstrikes across northern Venezuela, including the capital Caracas and captures Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro and first lady Cilia Flores.

events.title: Flash Airlines Flight 604
events.desc: Flash Airlines Flight 604 crashes into the Red Sea, resulting in 148 deaths, making it one of the deadliest aviation accidents in Egyptian history.

events.title: United States invasion of Panama
events.desc: United States invasion of Panama: Manuel Noriega, former leader of Panama, surrenders to American forces.

events.title: Aero Flight 311
events.desc: Aero Flight 311 crashes into the forest in Kvevlax, Finland, killing 25 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: US flying ace Major Greg "Pappy" Boyington is shot down in his Vought F4U Corsair by Captain Masajiro Kawato flying a Mitsubishi A6M Zero.

events.title: Siege of Sidney Street
events.desc: A gun battle in the East End of London leaves two dead. It sparked a political row over the involvement of then-Home Secretary Winston Churchill.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: American forces under General George Washington defeat British forces at the Battle of Princeton, helping boost patriot morale.

events.title: Berlingske
events.desc: The first issue of Berlingske, Denmark's oldest continually operating newspaper, is published.

events.title: Pope Leo X
events.desc: Pope Leo X excommunicates Martin Luther in the papal bull Decet Romanum Pontificem.

events.title: Decius
events.desc: Emperor Decius orders everyone in the Roman Empire (except Jews) to make sacrifices to the Roman gods.

births.name: Cicero
births.line: Roman philosopher, lawyer, and politician (died 43 BC)

births.name: Mel Gibson
births.line: American-Australian actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

deaths.text: Joseph Wirth, German educator and politician, Chancellor of Germany
Philip V of France, king of France

observances.text: Christian feast day: Holy Name of Jesus
Christian feast day: Kuriakose Elias Chavara (Syro-Malabar Catholic Church)
The tenth of the Twelve Days of Christmas (Western Christianity)
